name: Sweet Caroline — Section Drill
description: Drill each section of Neil Diamond's 'Sweet Caroline' with capo on the 2nd fret. A crowd-pleasing classic with simple changes and a big singalong chorus. Focus on dynamics between the quiet verse and the explosive chorus.

steps.name: Intro
steps.instructions: The intro builds on an A chord with the iconic descending riff. Strum A and let it ring, then walk down the riff notes on the A and D strings. Keep it simple and let the anticipation build.
steps.notes: The three-note 'ba ba ba' horn hits land on A. If you want to mimic the riff, play the notes on the 5th string: open A, 2nd fret B, 4th fret C#, then back down.

steps.name: Verse
steps.instructions: The verse stays on A for four measures, then moves to D for two and E for two. Keep the strumming relaxed and steady — the verse is conversational, building toward the pre-chorus.
steps.notes: 'Where it began, I can't begin to knowin'...' The long stretch on A means your strumming pattern needs to stay interesting. Vary your dynamics slightly to avoid going flat.

steps.name: Pre-Chorus
steps.instructions: The pre-chorus builds tension: A for two measures, then D for two, then E held for two with building intensity. This is the 'hands, touchin' hands' section — crescendo into the chorus.
steps.notes: 'Hands, touchin' hands, reachin' out, touchin' me, touchin' you...' Build your strum intensity through this section. The E at the end should be driving hard, setting up the big chorus hit.

steps.name: Chorus
steps.instructions: The chorus explodes on A D E — big open strums, maximum energy. The 'ba ba ba' hits are on A. Two measures per chord, let them ring out and sing along.
steps.notes: 'Sweet Caroline! BA BA BA! Good times never seemed so good!' This is the moment everyone sings along. Commit fully to the dynamics — the contrast with the verse is what makes this song work.

steps.name: Outro
steps.instructions: The outro repeats the chorus progression A D E D, gradually building to a final big A chord. Play through 2-3 cycles and end with a strong downstrum on A, letting it ring.
steps.notes: The crowd never wants this one to end. Keep the energy up through the repeats and finish strong on A.
